mappings.rationaleAI: Hinduism from Indus Valley through Vedic religion to contemporary practice addresses history (A), Vedic origins (A.3), modern Hinduism (A.9, A.10). Dharma, karma, moksha relate to beliefs (C.1.c), karman and samsara (C.1.c.ii), and three margas (C.1.c.iii). Rig Veda relates to scriptures (B.1.a), Vedanta to philosophy (C.2), caste to hierarchy (C.3.c).
Six orthodox schools with karma and moksha relate to Hindu philosophy (B.3) and karman and samsara (C.1.c.ii).
Sikhism from Guru Nanak directly addresses Sikhism's history (D.2.a), literature (D.2.b), and beliefs and practices (D.2.c).
Buddhism as heterodox challenge relates to the heterodox period (A.4).
The Buddha's relationship to Hinduism and Jainism relates to the heterodox period (A.4).
Gandhi's nonviolent resistance relates to modern Hinduism (A.9) and nationalist movements (C.2.f).
Contemporary India's caste dynamics relate to the caste system (C.3.c) and Hinduism today (A.10).
Ritual relates to Hindu practices (C.3) and sacrifice and worship (C.3.a).
Tibetan Buddhism's Indian origins relate to the heterodox period (A.4).
Spirituality across Hindu traditions relates to Hindu mysticism (B.4).
Provides comparative monotheistic context for understanding Hindu theistic and monistic traditions discussed in 823.C.1.a on views about God.
Theoretical approaches to myth relate to Hindu mythology (B.2).
Religion as global phenomenon provides context for the history of Hinduism (A).
Provides comparative context for understanding Eastern religious philosophy alongside the Hindu philosophical traditions of 823.B.3.
Pakistan's Indus Valley relates to protohistoric Indian religion (A.2), and partition relates to Sikhism (D.2.a.vi).
Covers epic as a global literary form including traditional heroic poetry from Asia with themes of heroism and warfare, relevant to post-Vedic Sanskrit epics such as the Mahabharata and Ramayana.

Covers monasticism including ascetic practices, monastic rules, and hagiographic literature across traditions, relevant to Jainism's ascetic background, Mahavira's teachings, later developments, Jain monastic figures, and institutional practices.
